channel.title: Lent Daily Reflections — XP-DEVOTION
channel.description: A 46-day journey through Lent with daily Scripture, reflection, prayer, and a simple spiritual activity to draw your heart closer to Christ.
channel.author: Eric Kouassi
channel.summary: Daily Lenten reflections to guide your heart through repentance, renewal, and deeper intimacy with God.

episodes.title: Ash Wednesday — Return to the Lord
episodes.description: “Return to Me with all your heart.” (Joel 2:12) Lent begins with a gentle invitation: come home. God does not call you back with fear but with tenderness. Today is not about proving anything—it is about turning your heart toward the One who has never stopped loving you.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: A gentle invitation to return to God with your whole heart.
episodes.summary: A quiet Ash Wednesday reflection on God’s tender call to return, rooted in Joel 2:12.

episodes.title: Thursday after Ash Wednesday — The Dust God Loves
episodes.description: “You are dust, and to dust you shall return.” (Genesis 3:19) Being dust is not insignificance—it is intimacy. You are formed and sustained by the God who breathed life into you. Lent teaches humility rooted in belovedness, not shame.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Humility rooted in God’s tender craftsmanship.
episodes.summary: A reflection on the humility of being dust—beloved, formed, and held by God.

episodes.title: Friday after Ash Wednesday — Choosing the Desert
episodes.description: “Jesus was led by the Spirit into the desert.” (Matthew 4:1) The desert is not punishment—it is preparation. Jesus entered the desert to be strengthened and clarified. Lent mirrors this journey, inviting you into a space where distractions fade and God becomes clearer.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Entering the desert with Jesus to find clarity and strength.
episodes.summary: A reflection on the desert as a place of preparation, courage, and spiritual clarity.

episodes.title: Saturday after Ash Wednesday — The First Step of Conversion
episodes.description: “Repent and believe in the Gospel.” (Mark 1:15) Conversion is not a single moment but a lifelong turning toward God. Lent invites you to take one step closer, trusting that God meets you with mercy, not judgment. Every small yes matters.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Conversion begins with one small yes.
episodes.summary: A reflection on conversion as a lifelong turning toward God, rooted in mercy and trust.

episodes.title: First Sunday of Lent — Fasting with Purpose
episodes.description: “Is this not the fast I choose: to loosen the bonds of injustice?” (Isaiah 58:6) Fasting is not about deprivation—it is about freedom. When you let go of something, you make space for God and compassion. True fasting opens your heart to others and aligns your life with God’s justice.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Fasting that opens the heart to God and others.
episodes.summary: A reflection on fasting as freedom, compassion, and alignment with God’s justice.

episodes.title: Monday of the First Week of Lent — The Quiet Work of Grace
episodes.description: “The seed grows, though he does not know how.” (Mark 4:27) Grace often works in hidden places. You may not see immediate change, but God is cultivating something deep within you. Trust the slow work of God, who transforms hearts gently and faithfully.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Trusting the slow, hidden work of grace.
episodes.summary: A reflection on God’s gentle and faithful transformation, even when unseen.

episodes.title: Tuesday of the First Week of Lent — Resting in God’s Mercy
episodes.description: “The Lord is merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.” (Psalm 103:8) God’s mercy is not earned—it is given. Lent is not about proving yourself but receiving the mercy that God delights to pour out. Rest in the truth that you are deeply loved.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Mercy that heals and restores.
episodes.summary: A reflection on receiving God’s mercy as gift, not achievement.

episodes.title: Wednesday of the First Week of Lent — Seeing with New Eyes
episodes.description: “Search me, O God, and know my heart.” (Psalm 139:23) Lent sharpens your spiritual vision. God gently reveals what distracts you and what draws you closer. This is not about shame but clarity—seeing your heart as God sees it.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Letting God reveal your heart with gentleness.
episodes.summary: A reflection on clarity, attention, and seeing as God sees.

episodes.title: Thursday of the First Week of Lent — Letting Go of False Strength
episodes.description: “My grace is sufficient for you.” (2 Corinthians 12:9) You don’t need to hold everything together. God meets you in weakness and fills you with His strength. Lent invites you to surrender the illusion of control and trust the One who carries you.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Strength found in surrender.
episodes.summary: A reflection on releasing false strength and trusting God’s sufficiency.

episodes.title: Friday of the First Week of Lent — The Gift of Hunger
episodes.description: “My soul longs for You, O God.” (Psalm 42:1) Spiritual hunger is a sign of life. It is God stirring your heart, drawing you deeper. Do not fear your longing—let it lead you toward the One who satisfies.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Let your longing lead you to God.
episodes.summary: A reflection on spiritual hunger as a gift that draws you deeper into God.

episodes.title: Saturday of the First Week of Lent — Love Your Enemies
episodes.description: “Love your enemies, and pray for those who persecute you.” (Matthew 5:44) Loving difficult people is not about approving their actions but choosing mercy over bitterness. Jesus invites you to pray for those who hurt you so your heart can be healed and freed.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Mercy that frees the heart.
episodes.summary: A reflection on choosing mercy over bitterness and praying for those who hurt you.

episodes.title: Second Sunday of Lent — Listen to Him
episodes.description: “This is my beloved Son. Listen to Him.” (Mark 9:7) The Transfiguration reveals Jesus in His radiant glory and invites you to listen beyond the noise of life. When you step into stillness, God brings clarity, direction, and a renewed vision of who Christ truly is.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Hearing Christ’s voice in stillness.
episodes.summary: A reflection on listening to Jesus with renewed clarity and trust.

episodes.title: Monday of the Second Week of Lent — Be Merciful
episodes.description: “Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.” (Luke 6:36) Mercy is not weakness but the strength to love beyond hurt. Jesus invites you to see others as God sees them—beloved, wounded, and worthy of compassion. When you choose mercy, you reflect the heart of the Father.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Mercy that reflects the Father’s heart.
episodes.summary: A reflection on choosing mercy as strength, seeing others as God sees them.

episodes.title: Tuesday of the Second Week of Lent — Humility
episodes.description: “Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.” (Matthew 23:12) Humility is not weakness but truth — seeing yourself as God sees you. It frees you from pressure, pride, and fear, making room for God to guide, strengthen, and transform your heart.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Humility that opens the heart to God.
episodes.summary: A reflection on humility as truth, freedom, and openness to God’s transforming love.

episodes.title: Wednesday of the Second Week of Lent — The Cost of Discipleship
episodes.description: “The Son of Man did not come to be served but to serve.” (Mark 10:45) Discipleship is not about comfort or recognition but about becoming like Christ through humble service. When you choose compassion over convenience and sacrifice over self‑importance, you discover the freedom and fullness that come from following Jesus’ way.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Following Christ through humble service.
episodes.summary: A reflection on discipleship as service, sacrifice, and becoming like Christ.

episodes.title: Thursday of the Second Week of Lent — Made New by Mercy
episodes.description: “Though your sins be like scarlet, they shall become white as snow.” (Isaiah 1:18) God’s mercy is not a threat but an invitation. He sees every flaw and every wound, yet He speaks renewal, healing, and hope. Nothing in you is beyond His ability to make new.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Mercy that renews and restores.
episodes.summary: A reflection on God’s renewing mercy that transforms what feels stained or broken.

episodes.title: Friday of the Second Week of Lent — From Rejection to Renewal
episodes.description: “The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.” (Psalm 118:22) God sees what others overlook. The places where you feel rejected or unseen are often the very places where He is preparing to build something new. In His hands, what feels discarded becomes essential and strong.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: God builds new life from rejected places.
episodes.summary: A reflection on God’s ability to transform rejection into strength and renewal.

episodes.title: Saturday of the Second Week of Lent — The Father Who Runs
episodes.description: “While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion.” (Luke 15:20) God is not distant or hesitant—He runs toward you with compassion the moment your heart turns toward Him. No matter how far you feel, His mercy restores you fully, joyfully, and tenderly.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: The Father runs to meet you with compassion.
episodes.summary: A reflection on God’s joyful, restoring mercy that meets you the moment you turn toward Him.

episodes.title: Third Sunday of Lent — Living Water
episodes.description: “Whoever drinks the water I give will never thirst.” (John 4:14) Jesus meets you at the well of your daily life, offering living water that satisfies your deepest thirst. Lent invites you to drink deeply of His presence, letting Him refresh what feels dry or weary.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Jesus offers water that satisfies the soul.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s living water that refreshes and restores the weary heart.

episodes.title: Monday of the Third Week of Lent — The One Thing Needed
episodes.description: “There is need of only one thing.” (Luke 10:42) Jesus invites you to let go of pressure and return to what matters most: His presence. When you choose stillness over striving and listen before doing, your heart becomes anchored and your spirit finds rest.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Returning to what matters most: Christ’s presence.
episodes.summary: A reflection on choosing stillness and presence over pressure and striving.

episodes.title: Tuesday of the Third Week of Lent — Learning to Trust
episodes.description: “Trust in the Lord with all your heart.” (Proverbs 3:5) Trust grows slowly, through small acts of surrender. Lent teaches you to lean not on your own understanding but on God’s faithful love. He guides your steps even when the path is unclear.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Trust grows through small acts of surrender.
episodes.summary: A reflection on trusting God’s faithful guidance even when the path is unclear.

episodes.title: Wednesday of the Third Week of Lent — The Law of Love
episodes.description: “Love God with all your heart… and love your neighbor as yourself.” (Matthew 22:37–39) Holiness is not complicated—it is love lived out in daily life. Lent calls you back to the heart of the Gospel: loving God fully and loving others generously.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Holiness expressed through love.
episodes.summary: A reflection on returning to the heart of the Gospel: love of God and neighbor.

episodes.title: Thursday of the Third Week of Lent — Listening to God’s Voice
episodes.description: “My sheep hear My voice.” (John 10:27) God speaks in many ways—through Scripture, silence, people, and the movements of your heart. Lent trains your ears to listen more closely, to recognize the Shepherd’s voice amid the noise.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Hearing the Shepherd’s voice in the noise.
episodes.summary: A reflection on listening for God’s voice through Scripture, silence, and the heart.

episodes.title: Friday of the Third Week of Lent — A Heart of Compassion
episodes.description: “Clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience.” (Colossians 3:12) Compassion is the heartbeat of Christ. Lent invites you to see others with His eyes—to notice their pain, their dignity, their worth. Compassion transforms not only others but your own heart.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Seeing others with Christ’s compassion.
episodes.summary: A reflection on compassion as the heartbeat of Christ and a path to transformation.

episodes.title: Saturday of the Third Week of Lent — The Call to Holiness
episodes.description: “Be holy, for I am holy.” (1 Peter 1:16) Holiness is not perfection—it is belonging to God. Lent reminds you that you are set apart for love, mercy, and goodness. God calls you to reflect His heart in the world.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Holiness as belonging to God.
episodes.summary: A reflection on holiness as living God’s love, mercy, and goodness in the world.

episodes.title: Fourth Sunday of Lent — Rejoice in the Light
episodes.description: “I am the light of the world.” (John 9:5) Laetare Sunday is a pause of joy in the desert of Lent. God reminds you that the journey leads to light, not darkness. Christ shines into every shadow, bringing hope and renewal.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Joy in the midst of Lent’s desert.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s light that brings hope and renewal during Lent’s journey.

episodes.title: Monday of the Fourth Week of Lent — A New Heart
episodes.description: “I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit within you.” (Ezekiel 36:26) God does not simply repair your heart—He renews it. Lent is a season of inner transformation, where God replaces what is hardened with what is alive, tender, and responsive to His love.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Let God renew your heart.
episodes.summary: A reflection on God’s promise to give a new heart and renew the spirit within.

episodes.title: Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Lent — Healing Waters
episodes.description: “Do you want to be made well?” (John 5:6) Jesus meets you at the places where you feel stuck. His question is gentle but piercing: Do you desire healing? Lent invites you to open the places you hide, trusting that Christ’s touch restores what is wounded.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Let Christ meet you where you feel stuck.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s healing question and the invitation to open wounded places to Him.

episodes.title: Wednesday of the Fourth Week of Lent — Light in the Darkness
episodes.description: “I am the light of the world.” (John 8:12) Even in seasons of darkness, Christ shines. His light does not shame you—it guides you. Lent teaches you to walk toward the light, trusting that no darkness is too deep for His love.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Christ’s light guides without shaming.
episodes.summary: A reflection on trusting Christ’s light even in seasons of darkness.

episodes.title: Thursday of the Fourth Week of Lent — The God Who Provides
episodes.description: “My God will supply all your needs.” (Philippians 4:19) God knows your needs before you speak them. Lent invites you to trust His provision, even when you cannot see the path ahead. His faithfulness is your foundation.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Trusting God’s faithful provision.
episodes.summary: A reflection on trusting God’s provision and faithfulness in every need.

episodes.title: Friday of the Fourth Week of Lent — A Heart of Sacrifice
episodes.description: “Take up your cross daily and follow Me.” (Luke 9:23) Sacrifice is not about suffering for its own sake—it is about love. Jesus invites you to carry your cross with Him, trusting that every sacrifice offered in love bears fruit.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Sacrifice offered in love bears fruit.
episodes.summary: A reflection on carrying your cross with Christ and offering sacrifice in love.

episodes.title: Saturday of the Fourth Week of Lent — The Promise of New Life
episodes.description: “I am the resurrection and the life.” (John 11:25) Jesus brings life where there seems to be none. Lent prepares your heart for resurrection—new beginnings, renewed hope, restored joy. Nothing is beyond God’s power to revive.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Christ brings new life where hope seems lost.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s power to revive what feels dead within us.

episodes.title: Fifth Sunday of Lent — A Heart Set Free
episodes.description: “If the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.” (John 8:36) Freedom in Christ is not abstract—it is deeply personal. Jesus frees you from fear, shame, and sin. Lent invites you to walk in that freedom with confidence and joy.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Christ frees the heart to live in joy.
episodes.summary: A reflection on the personal freedom Christ offers and the invitation to walk in it.

episodes.title: Monday of the Fifth Week of Lent — Mercy Over Judgment
episodes.description: “Neither do I condemn you.” (John 8:11) Jesus meets you with mercy, not condemnation. His forgiveness restores dignity and opens the door to new beginnings. Lent teaches you to receive mercy and extend it to others.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Mercy restores dignity and opens new beginnings.
episodes.summary: A reflection on receiving Christ’s mercy and offering it freely to others.

episodes.title: Tuesday of the Fifth Week of Lent — Faith That Endures
episodes.description: “Fix your eyes on Jesus.” (Hebrews 12:2) Endurance grows when your focus is on Christ. Lent strengthens your faith by teaching you to look beyond your struggles and fix your gaze on the One who leads you.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Endurance grows when Christ is your focus.
episodes.summary: A reflection on persevering by fixing your eyes on Jesus.

episodes.title: Wednesday of the Fifth Week of Lent — The God Who Remembers
episodes.description: “I have engraved you on the palms of My hands.” (Isaiah 49:16) God never forgets you. Even when you feel unseen or overlooked, He holds you close. Lent reminds you that you are always in God’s heart and always in His hands.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: You are always in God’s heart and hands.
episodes.summary: A reflection on God’s faithful remembrance and tender care.

episodes.title: Thursday of the Fifth Week of Lent — Preparing the Heart
episodes.description: “A broken and contrite heart, O God, You will not despise.” (Psalm 51:17) As Lent nears its end, God invites you to prepare your heart for the mystery of the Passion. A humble heart is the soil where grace takes root.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: A humble heart prepares for the Passion.
episodes.summary: A reflection on preparing the heart with humility as Lent approaches Holy Week.

episodes.title: Friday of the Fifth Week of Lent — The Cost of Love
episodes.description: “The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.” (John 10:11) Love always costs something. Jesus shows the depth of His love by laying down His life. Lent invites you to contemplate this love that holds nothing back.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Love that holds nothing back.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s sacrificial love revealed in the Good Shepherd.

episodes.title: Saturday of the Fifth Week of Lent — Waiting with Hope
episodes.description: “I wait for the Lord, my soul waits.” (Psalm 130:5) Waiting is not passive—it is trust in motion. Lent teaches you to wait with hope, knowing that God is faithful and His promises are sure.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Waiting as trust in motion.
episodes.summary: A reflection on waiting with hope and trusting God’s faithful timing.

episodes.title: Palm Sunday of the Passion of the Lord — Behold Your King
episodes.description: “Hosanna to the Son of David!” (Matthew 21:9) Palm Sunday begins the great mystery of Holy Week. Jesus enters Jerusalem not as a warrior but as a humble King. Lent now leads you to the threshold of the Passion, where love is revealed in its fullness.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Entering Holy Week with the humble King.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s humble kingship and the beginning of Holy Week.

episodes.title: Monday of Holy Week — Extravagant Love
episodes.description: “Mary anointed the feet of Jesus.” (John 12:3) Mary’s act of anointing Jesus is a gesture of pure devotion. Holy Week invites you to offer Jesus your own costly love—your time, your attention, your heart.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Offering Jesus your costly love.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Mary’s devotion and the invitation to offer Jesus your whole heart.

episodes.title: Tuesday of Holy Week — Walking in the Light
episodes.description: “Walk while you have the light.” (John 12:35) Holy Week calls you to walk closely with Jesus, choosing the light even when shadows fall. Christ’s light guides your steps and strengthens your heart for the journey ahead.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Walking with Christ in the light of Holy Week.
episodes.summary: A reflection on choosing Christ’s light and walking with Him through Holy Week.

episodes.title: Wednesday of Holy Week — Betrayal and Fidelity
episodes.description: Holy Week reveals both human frailty and divine fidelity. Even as betrayal unfolds, Jesus remains steadfast in love. This day invites you to stay close to Christ, trusting His unwavering heart.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Staying close to Christ’s unwavering heart.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s fidelity in the midst of human betrayal.

episodes.title: Holy Thursday — The Gift of the Eucharist
episodes.description: “This is My body, given for you.” (Luke 22:19) On Holy Thursday, Jesus gives Himself completely in the Eucharist. Love becomes a meal, a gift, a presence that remains. Tonight invites you to receive Him with awe, gratitude, and intimacy.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Love becomes a meal, a gift, a presence.
episodes.summary: A reflection on the Eucharist as Christ’s total gift of Himself.

episodes.title: Good Friday — Love Poured Out
episodes.description: “It is finished.” (John 19:30) Good Friday reveals the depth of Christ’s love. He holds nothing back, embracing the cross for your salvation. Today is a day of silence, awe, and gratitude for the love that saves the world.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: The depth of Christ’s saving love.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Christ’s sacrifice and the love revealed on the cross.

episodes.title: Holy Saturday — The Great Silence
episodes.description: Holy Saturday is the quiet between death and resurrection. Christ rests in the tomb, and all creation waits. This silence is not emptiness—it is anticipation. God is at work in hidden places, preparing for the dawn of new life.
episodes.author: XP-DEVOTION
episodes.subtitle: Waiting in the holy silence before resurrection.
episodes.summary: A reflection on Holy Saturday’s quiet hope and God’s hidden work.
